🌐 CS Class 12 (083) — Unit 2: Chapter 8 – Computer Networks

🌐
CBSE Class XII · Computer Science Code 083 · Unit 2 · Chapter 8

Computer Networks

Evolution of networking, Network types, Topologies, Network devices, Web services, HTTP, WWW, DNS, URLs.

Topic 1 Network Types & Topologies

▼
📖 Computer Network A group of connected computing devices (computers, printers, smartphones) that can communicate and share hardware and software resources.
⭐ Types of Networks
TypeFull FormCoverageExample
PANPersonal Area Network1–10 metresBluetooth, USB
LANLocal Area NetworkBuilding / CampusSchool/Office network
MANMetropolitan Area NetworkCityCable TV network
WANWide Area NetworkCountry / WorldInternet
⭐ Network Topologies
TopologyStructureAdvantageDisadvantage
BusAll nodes on single cableSimple, low costSingle point of failure
StarAll nodes connected to central hubEasy fault detectionHub failure = network down
RingNodes in circular closed loopEqual accessOne failure breaks network
TreeHierarchical; bus + starScalableRoot failure = network down
MeshEvery node connected to every otherHighly reliableExpensive, complex wiring

Topic 2 Network Devices & Web Services

▼
⭐ Network Devices
DeviceFunction
HubConnects multiple devices; sends data to ALL nodes (broadcasts)
SwitchConnects devices in LAN; sends data only to the TARGET node
RouterConnects different networks; routes data packets between them
ModemConverts digital signals to analog (modulation) and back (demodulation)
GatewayInterface between two different network protocols
RepeaterAmplifies/regenerates signal over long distances
NICNetwork Interface Card — connects device to network
⭐ Web Services — Key Terms
TermDefinition
WWWWorld Wide Web — system of interlinked hypertext documents accessed via Internet
URLUniform Resource Locator — address of a web resource (e.g. https://example.com)
DNSDomain Name System — translates domain names to IP addresses
Web BrowserSoftware to access web pages (Chrome, Firefox)
Web ServerComputer that stores and serves website content
Web HostingService providing resources to store and serve websites on the Internet

📡 CS Class 12 (083) — Unit 2: Chapter 9 – Data Communication

📡
CBSE Class XII · Computer Science Code 083 · Unit 2 · Chapter 9

Data Communication

Communication components, Bandwidth, IP Address, Switching, Transmission Media and Network Protocols.

Topic 1 Data Communication — Basics

▼
⭐ Components of Data Communication
ComponentRole
SenderDevice that sends data
ReceiverDevice that receives data
MessageThe data being communicated
MediumPath through which data travels (wired/wireless)
ProtocolSet of rules governing communication
⭐ Key Terms
TermDefinition
BandwidthMaximum data transfer rate of a communication medium (bps, Kbps, Mbps, Gbps)
IP AddressUnique numerical address (32-bit IPv4 or 128-bit IPv6) assigned to each device on a network
Circuit SwitchingDedicated path established for the entire duration of communication (e.g. telephone)
Packet SwitchingData broken into packets; each packet may take different route (e.g. Internet)

Topic 2 Transmission Media & Protocols

▼
⭐ Transmission Media
TypeMediumFeature
WiredTwisted Pair CableCheapest; used in telephone and LAN
Co-axial CableBetter shielding; used in cable TV
Fibre Optic CableFastest; uses light signals; immune to interference
WirelessRadio WavesLong range; used in FM radio, WiFi
MicrowavesLine-of-sight; used in mobile networks
InfraredShort range; used in TV remotes
⭐ Network Protocols
ProtocolFull FormUse
HTTPHyperText Transfer ProtocolTransfer web pages
HTTPSHTTP SecureEncrypted web browsing
FTPFile Transfer ProtocolTransfer files
SMTPSimple Mail Transfer ProtocolSend emails
POP3Post Office Protocol 3Receive emails
TCP/IPTransmission Control Protocol / IPCore Internet protocol
TELNETTeletype NetworkRemote login to systems
VoIPVoice over Internet ProtocolVoice calls over Internet

🔒 CS Class 12 (083) — Unit 2: Chapter 10 – Security Aspects

🔒
CBSE Class XII · Computer Science Code 083 · Unit 2 · Chapter 10

Security Aspects

Network threats, Malware types, Firewall, Cookies, Hackers — complete notes.

Topic 1 Network Threats & Malware

▼
⭐ Types of Malware
MalwareDefinition
VirusSelf-replicating program that attaches to legitimate files and spreads when files are shared
WormSelf-replicating program that spreads through networks without attaching to files
Trojan HorseAppears as useful software but contains malicious code; does not self-replicate
RansomwareEncrypts victim's data and demands ransom for decryption key
SpywareSecretly monitors user activity and sends information to attackers
AdwareDisplays unwanted advertisements automatically
⭐ Types of Hackers
TypeDescription
White HatEthical hacker; uses skills to improve security with permission
Black HatMalicious hacker; breaks into systems illegally for personal gain
Grey HatBreaks into systems without permission but without malicious intent

Topic 2 Firewall, Cookies & Security Measures

▼
📖 Firewall A network security system (hardware or software) that monitors and controls incoming and outgoing network traffic based on predefined security rules. Acts as a barrier between trusted internal network and untrusted external networks.
📖 Cookies Small text files stored on the user's computer by websites. Used to remember user preferences, login sessions and browsing history.
⭐ Security Measures
  • Antivirus Software — Detects and removes malware.
  • Encryption — Converting data into unreadable form to prevent unauthorized access.
  • Strong Passwords — Using complex passwords and changing them regularly.
  • Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) — Extra layer of security beyond password.
  • Regular Updates — Keeping OS and software up to date to patch vulnerabilities.
📚 Chapters
🐍 CS Class 12
Ch 1 – Python Revision Tour Ch 2 – Exception Handling Ch 3 – File Handling Ch 4 – Stack Ch 5 – Queue Ch 6 – Sorting Algorithms Ch 7 – Searching Algorithms Ch 8 – Computer Networks Ch 9 – Data Communication Ch 10 – Security Aspects Ch 11 – Understanding Data Ch 12 – Database Concepts Ch 13 – SQL

📊 CS Class 12 (083) — Unit 3: Chapter 11 – Understanding Data

📊
CBSE Class XII · Computer Science Code 083 · Unit 3 · Chapter 11

Understanding Data

Data, Information, Types of Data, Data Collection, Open Data and Big Data basics.

Topic 1 Data & Information

▼
⭐ Data vs Information
DataInformation
Raw, unprocessed facts and figuresProcessed, organized and meaningful data
No direct meaning on its ownHas meaning and context
Example: 85, 72, 90Example: Marks scored by students in a test
⭐ Types of Data
TypeDescriptionExample
QuantitativeNumerical data that can be measuredHeight, Weight, Marks
QualitativeDescriptive, non-numerical dataColor, Name, Gender
StructuredOrganized in rows and columnsDatabase tables, Spreadsheets
UnstructuredNo predefined formatImages, Videos, Emails

Topic 2 Data Collection & Open Data

▼
⭐ Methods of Data Collection
  • Surveys / Questionnaires — Collecting data directly from individuals.
  • Sensors / IoT Devices — Automatic data collection from environment.
  • Web Scraping — Extracting data from websites automatically.
  • Transactions — Records from purchases, banking, etc.
  • Social Media — User posts, likes, comments and activity.
📖 Open Data Data that is freely available to everyone to access, use and redistribute without restrictions. Published by governments, research organizations and NGOs to promote transparency.
⭐ Data Formats
FormatDescription
CSVComma-Separated Values — simple tabular data
JSONJavaScript Object Notation — web APIs and configs
XMLeXtensible Markup Language — structured data exchange
📚 Chapters
🐍 CS Class 12
Ch 1 – Python Revision Tour Ch 2 – Exception Handling Ch 3 – File Handling Ch 4 – Stack Ch 5 – Queue Ch 6 – Sorting Algorithms Ch 7 – Searching Algorithms Ch 8 – Computer Networks Ch 9 – Data Communication Ch 10 – Security Aspects Ch 11 – Understanding Data Ch 12 – Database Concepts Ch 13 – SQL

🗄️ CS Class 12 (083) — Unit 3: Chapter 12 – Database Concepts

🗄️
CBSE Class XII · Computer Science Code 083 · Unit 3 · Chapter 12

Database Concepts

DBMS, RDBMS, Relation, Tuple, Attribute, Keys — Primary, Candidate, Foreign, Alternate.

Topic 1 Database, DBMS & RDBMS

▼
📖 Database An organized collection of interrelated data stored in a structured format, designed for easy access, management and updating.
📖 DBMS Database Management System — software that enables users to create, access, update and manage databases. Examples: MySQL, Oracle, MS Access.
⭐ Advantages of DBMS
  • Reduces data redundancy (duplicate data).
  • Ensures data consistency and integrity.
  • Provides data security and access control.
  • Supports concurrent access by multiple users.
  • Easy backup and recovery.
⭐ RDBMS Terminologies
TermDefinition
RelationA table with rows and columns
TupleA row in a table (one record)
AttributeA column in a table (one field)
DomainSet of valid values for an attribute
DegreeNumber of columns (attributes) in a table
CardinalityNumber of rows (tuples) in a table

Topic 2 Keys in RDBMS

▼
⭐ Types of Keys
KeyDefinition
Candidate KeyMinimal set of attribute(s) that can uniquely identify each tuple. A table can have multiple candidate keys.
Primary KeyThe candidate key selected to uniquely identify each row. Cannot be NULL or duplicate.
Alternate KeyAll candidate keys that are NOT chosen as primary key.
Foreign KeyAttribute in one table that refers to the Primary Key of another table. Establishes relationship between tables.
📚 Chapters
🐍 CS Class 12
Ch 1 – Python Revision Tour Ch 2 – Exception Handling Ch 3 – File Handling Ch 4 – Stack Ch 5 – Queue Ch 6 – Sorting Algorithms Ch 7 – Searching Algorithms Ch 8 – Computer Networks Ch 9 – Data Communication Ch 10 – Security Aspects Ch 11 – Understanding Data Ch 12 – Database Concepts Ch 13 – SQL

🛢️ CS Class 12 (083) — Unit 3: Chapter 13 – SQL

🛢️
CBSE Class XII · Computer Science Code 083 · Unit 3 · Chapter 13

Structured Query Language (SQL)

DDL, DML, Data Types, Constraints, SELECT with WHERE/ORDER BY/GROUP BY, Aggregate Functions and Joins.

Topic 1 SQL Basics — DDL Commands

▼
📖 SQL Structured Query Language — a standard language used to create, manage and manipulate relational databases.
⭐ SQL Data Types
Data TypeUse
INT / INTEGERWhole numbers
FLOAT / DECIMALDecimal numbers
CHAR(n)Fixed-length string of n characters
VARCHAR(n)Variable-length string up to n characters
DATEDate in YYYY-MM-DD format
⭐ DDL Commands (Data Definition Language)
-- Create database
CREATE DATABASE school;
USE school;

-- Create table
CREATE TABLE student (
  rno INT PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL,
  marks FLOAT,
  grade CHAR(1)
);

-- Modify table
ALTER TABLE student ADD city VARCHAR(20);
ALTER TABLE student MODIFY marks INT;
ALTER TABLE student DROP COLUMN city;

-- Delete table / database
DROP TABLE student;
DROP DATABASE school;

Topic 2 DML Commands — INSERT, SELECT, UPDATE, DELETE

▼
⭐ DML Commands (Data Manipulation Language)
-- Insert record
INSERT INTO student VALUES (1,'Raj',95,'A');

-- Select records
SELECT * FROM student;
SELECT name, marks FROM student WHERE marks > 80;
SELECT * FROM student ORDER BY marks DESC;

-- Update record
UPDATE student SET marks=90 WHERE rno=1;

-- Delete record
DELETE FROM student WHERE rno=1;
⭐ SQL Clauses
ClausePurposeExample
WHEREFilter rows based on conditionWHERE marks > 80
ORDER BYSort result (ASC/DESC)ORDER BY name ASC
DISTINCTRemove duplicate valuesSELECT DISTINCT grade
LIKEPattern matching (% = any chars, _ = one char)WHERE name LIKE 'R%'
BETWEENRange condition (inclusive)WHERE marks BETWEEN 70 AND 90
INMatch from a list of valuesWHERE grade IN ('A','B')
IS NULLCheck for NULL valuesWHERE marks IS NULL
GROUP BYGroup rows with same valueGROUP BY grade
HAVINGFilter groups (used with GROUP BY)HAVING COUNT(*) > 2

Topic 3 Aggregate Functions, Constraints & Joins

▼
⭐ Aggregate Functions
FunctionPurposeExample
COUNT()Counts rowsSELECT COUNT(*) FROM student;
SUM()Sum of valuesSELECT SUM(marks) FROM student;
AVG()Average of valuesSELECT AVG(marks) FROM student;
MAX()Maximum valueSELECT MAX(marks) FROM student;
MIN()Minimum valueSELECT MIN(marks) FROM student;
⭐ SQL Constraints
ConstraintPurpose
NOT NULLField cannot be left empty
UNIQUEAll values in column must be different
PRIMARY KEYNOT NULL + UNIQUE; uniquely identifies each row
FOREIGN KEYLinks a field to Primary Key of another table
DEFAULTSets a default value when no value is provided
CHECKEnsures value satisfies a condition
⭐ Joins
Join TypeDescription
Cartesian ProductAll combinations of rows from two tables (no condition)
Equi JoinJoin using equality condition on common columns
Natural JoinEqui join that removes duplicate columns automatically
-- Natural Join example
SELECT * FROM student NATURAL JOIN fees;
📚 Chapters
☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

🌐 IT 802 Class 12 — Unit 2: Operating Web Based Applications

🌐
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part B · Unit 2

Operating Web Based Applications

Web Based Applications, Online Reservation System, E-Governance, Online Shopping, Online Courses, Project Management and Phases of Web Application Project.

📚 7 Topics ✅ Full Syllabus 🌐 Web Applications

Topic 1 Web Based Applications

▼
📖 Web Based Application Applications that can be accessed over a computer network or the Internet using a web browser. They do not need to be installed on the user's device.
⭐ Examples of Web Based Applications
  • Online Reservation System
  • Online Shopping and Bill Payments
  • Online Tutorials and Courses
  • E-Governance

Topic 2 Online Reservation System

▼
📖 Online Reservation System A system that allows booking and cancellation of tickets through the Internet. A user can retrieve information and perform transactions related to reservations.
⭐ Examples of Online Reservation
  • Airline ticket booking
  • Railway ticket booking
  • Bus ticket booking
  • Movie ticket booking
  • Hotel booking
  • Car rental and Tour booking
⭐ Advantages for Consumers
  • Can compare prices and get the best deal.
  • Can book from anywhere — home, office or while travelling.
  • Fast and secure.
  • Freedom to choose seats, food, and arrangements.
  • Saves time compared to standing in queues.
  • Access to special discounts like advance booking and seasonal offers.
⭐ Advantages for Service Providers
  • Less manpower required as tasks are automated.
  • Physical office location does not matter.
  • Services available to anyone globally with Internet access.
⭐ Precautions During Online Transactions
  • Use complex passwords — mix uppercase, numbers and special characters.
  • Do not share passwords with anyone.
  • Never use names of relatives or friends as passwords.

Topic 3 E-Governance

▼
📖 E-Governance The use of information and communication technology (ICT) tools by the government to provide services and information to citizens. The objective is to bring transparency and provide quality services to citizens.
⭐ Categories of E-Governance Services
CategoryDescription
Online ServicesPassport, PAN card, tax filing, certificates
Online InformationGovernment policies, schemes, regulations
⭐ E-Governance Initiatives
  • National e-Government Plan (NeGP)
  • National e-Governance Division (NeGD)
  • Digital India Programme
  • Citizens Services, Business Services, Government Services
⭐ Important E-Governance Websites
  • india.gov.in
  • goidirectory.nic.in
  • uidai.gov.in
  • digitalindia.gov.in
  • mygov.in

Topic 4 Online Shopping & Bill Payments

▼
📖 Online Shopping An e-commerce application where customers can purchase goods over the Internet without visiting a physical store.
⭐ Benefits of Online Shopping
  • Saves time when the customer cannot visit a store.
  • Useful when the required store is far away.
  • Eliminates travel and parking costs.
  • Access to products not available in local markets.
  • Enables delivery to someone at a different location.
⭐ Online Bill Payment Methods
  • Debit Card
  • Credit Card
  • Net Banking
  • Wire Transfer
  • UPI Apps — PhonePe, Paytm, Google Pay

Topic 5 Online Courses

▼
📖 Online Courses Learning programmes delivered over the Internet where students and teachers are at different physical locations, connected via the Internet. Also called e-learning or online tutoring.
⭐ Benefits of Online Courses
  • Freedom to learn from any location.
  • Freedom to learn at any convenient time.
  • Learner can study at their own pace.
  • No discrimination based on age, caste, gender or qualification.
  • Enriched with audio and video content, making learning engaging.
  • Available for almost all topics, sometimes in regional languages.
⭐ Popular Online Educational Websites
  • khanacademy.org
  • udacity.org
  • coursera.org
  • edx.org
  • w3schools.com
  • ncert.nic.in

Topic 6 Project Management

▼
📖 Project Management The process of achieving the objectives of a project through a set of activities defined within a time frame to produce the desired end result. It is the application of knowledge, skills, tools and techniques to meet project requirements.
📖 Project A task undertaken to create a unique product, service or result.
⭐ Characteristics of a Project
  • Has a definite beginning and an end.
  • Requires finite resources to complete.
  • Has a specific time frame with definite start and end dates.
  • Is complete when its objectives are achieved.
📖 Web Based Application A program stored on a remote server, accessible online via a web browser. It can be a quiz, a game or a calculator.

Topic 7 Phases in a Web Application Project

▼
⭐ Four Phases of a Web Application Project
PhaseFocusActivities
1. Requirements DefinitionWhat to build?Identify problem, define scope, check technical and economic feasibility
2. DesignHow to build?Design database structure, page structure and relationships between pages
3. ImplementationBuildingCreate frontend, backend and connectivity between them
4. TestingVerificationTest with multiple datasets, fix bugs and ensure application is ready to release

☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

☕ IT 802 Class 12 — Unit 3: Java Programming

☕
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part B · Unit 3

Fundamentals of Java Programming

Introduction to Java, Data Types, Variables, Operators, Control Flow, Arrays, OOP, String Methods, Exception Handling, Assertions, Threads and Wrapper Classes.

📚 8 Topics ✅ Full Syllabus 💻 Code Examples

Topic 1 Introduction to Java

▼
📖 Java A popular high-level, Object Oriented Programming language used to develop a wide variety of applications including desktop, mobile, web and database applications.
⭐ Key Java Concepts
TermDefinition
JVMJava Virtual Machine — interprets byte code and executes it. Enables "Write Once, Run Anywhere".
Byte CodeHighly optimized intermediate code produced when a Java program is compiled. Stored as a .class file and run by JVM.
Java IDEIntegrated Development Environment — includes text editor, compiler, debugger and form designer (e.g. NetBeans).
CommentNon-executable text added for documentation. Written as // single line or /* multi-line */.

Topic 2 Variables, Data Types & Input

▼
📖 Variable A named storage location in memory whose value can change during program execution. Declared as: datatype variableName;
⭐ Java Primitive Data Types
Data TypeSizeExample
int4 bytesint x = 10;
float4 bytesfloat pi = 3.14f;
double8 bytesdouble d = 3.14;
char2 byteschar c = 'A';
boolean1 bitboolean b = true;
byte1 bytebyte b = 127;
short2 bytesshort s = 100;
long8 byteslong l = 100L;
⭐ Rules for Naming Variables
  • Must begin with a letter, underscore (_) or dollar sign ($).
  • Must be one word — spaces not allowed.
  • Cannot be a Java reserved keyword.
  • Case-sensitive — Name and name are different.
⭐ Taking Input from User — Scanner Class
import java.util.Scanner;

Scanner sc = new Scanner(System.in);
String name = sc.next();
String s = sc.next();
int num = Integer.parseInt(s); // String to int

Topic 3 Control Flow — Selection & Repetition

▼
⭐ Selection Statements
StatementUse
if-elseExecute a block based on True/False condition
switchSelect one block from multiple choices based on a value
if (condition) {
  // statements
} else {
  // statements
}
⭐ Repetition (Loop) Statements
LoopWhen to UseKey Feature
whileNumber of repetitions unknownChecks condition BEFORE executing body
do-whileNumber of repetitions unknownChecks condition AFTER executing body — runs at least once
forNumber of repetitions knownMost commonly used loop
// for loop syntax
for (init; condition; update) {
  // statements
}

Topic 4 Arrays & User Defined Functions

▼
📖 Array A variable that can hold more than one value of the same data type. Elements are stored in contiguous memory and identified by an index starting from 0.
⭐ Array Syntax
// Declare and create array
int[] marks = new int[5];

// Access elements
marks[0] = 90;
System.out.println(marks[0]);
📖 Method (Function) A named block of statements that performs a specific task and can be called multiple times. May accept parameters and may return a value.
⭐ Method Structure
returnType methodName(parameters) {
  // body
  return value; // optional
}

// Example
int add(int a, int b) {
  return a + b;
}

Topic 5 Object Oriented Programming (OOP)

▼
⭐ Key OOP Concepts
ConceptDefinition
ClassA template or blueprint that defines attributes (data members) and behaviour (methods). To use it, an object must be created.
ObjectAn instance of a class. Multiple objects can be created from one class, each having the same data structure.
ConstructorA special method with the same name as the class. No return type. Used to initialize data members. Called automatically when an object is created.
⭐ Access Modifiers
ModifierScope
privateAccessible only within the same class. Used for data members to ensure security.
publicAccessible from anywhere. Used for methods to make them available outside the class.
class Student {
  private int rno;
  public String name;

  // Constructor
  Student(int r, String n) {
    rno = r; name = n;
  }
}

// Creating object
Student s = new Student(1, "Raj");

Topic 6 String Manipulation

▼
⭐ Important Java String Methods
MethodDescriptionExampleOutput
length()Returns length of string"Java".length()4
charAt(i)Returns character at index i"Java".charAt(2)'v'
substring(s,e)Returns part of string"Hello".substring(1,4)"ell"
concat(str)Concatenates two strings"Java".concat("123")"Java123"
equals(str)Checks exact match (case-sensitive)"java".equals("Java")false
equalsIgnoreCase()Compares ignoring case"java".equalsIgnoreCase("Java")true
toUpperCase()Converts to uppercase"java".toUpperCase()"JAVA"
toLowerCase()Converts to lowercase"JAVA".toLowerCase()"java"
trim()Removes leading/trailing spaces" Java ".trim()"Java"
replace(a,b)Replaces character a with b"Java".replace('a','@')"J@v@"
indexOf(c)Index of first occurrence"Java".indexOf('v')2
contains(str)Checks if string contains sequence"Java123".contains("123")true
startsWith(str)Checks if starts with given string"Java".startsWith("Ja")true
endsWith(str)Checks if ends with given string"Hello.java".endsWith(".java")true

Topic 7 Exception Handling & Assertions

▼
📖 Exception Handling A mechanism to identify exceptions (runtime errors) and perform corrective actions so the program can continue or terminate gracefully.
⭐ try-catch-finally Syntax
try {
  // code that may throw exception
}
catch (ExceptionType e) {
  // handle exception
}
finally {
  // always executes
}
⭐ Clauses Summary
ClauseExecutes When
tryAlways — wraps the risky code
catchOnly when exception is raised in try block
finallyAlways — used for cleanup tasks
📖 Assertion A debugging mechanism used to detect and correct logical errors during development. Written as:
assert condition; or assert condition : "Error Message";

Topic 8 Threads & Wrapper Classes

▼
📖 Thread The smallest unit of execution within a process. Allows multiple tasks to run concurrently to improve performance and responsiveness.
⭐ Two Ways to Create a Thread in Java
MethodHow
Extending Thread classCreate a class that extends Thread and override the run() method
Implementing Runnable interfaceCreate a class that implements Runnable and override run(). More flexible as it allows extending other classes too.
// Method 1: Extending Thread
class MyThread extends Thread {
  public void run() {
    System.out.println("Thread running");
  }
}
📖 Wrapper Class A class that converts a primitive data type into an object. Each primitive type has a corresponding wrapper class.
⭐ Primitive vs Wrapper Class
PrimitiveWrapper Class
intInteger
floatFloat
doubleDouble
charCharacter
booleanBoolean
byteByte
shortShort
longLong

☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

💼 IT 802 Class 12 — Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning IT – DMA

💼
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part B · Unit 4

Work Integrated Learning IT – DMA

Work areas using DBMS, applications of database in Education, Banking, Hospitals, Government, Companies, E-Commerce, Airlines, Railways, Telecom and Hotels.

📚 3 Topics ✅ Full Syllabus 💼 Real World DB

Topic 1 Work Integrated Learning — Definition

▼
📖 Work Integrated Learning (WIL) An approach that integrates academic learning with its real-time practical applications. The main objective is to support experiential learning and develop decision-making and problem-solving abilities in students.
📖 Work Integrated Learning IT Integrating academic learning with real-time practical applications in the field of Information Technology.

Topic 2 Work Areas Using DBMS

▼
⭐ Domains That Use Database Management System
DomainHow DBMS is Used
EducationStoring student details, marks, results; faculty records; school/college infrastructure and course details.
BankingStoring customer personal details, account and loan information, daily transaction records; employee details, salary and leave records.
HospitalsMaintaining patient personal details, health records, hospitalization dates; storing doctor, nurse and staff information; tracking rooms, equipment and medicines.
Government SectorElectoral rolls, tax records (income, sales, house), criminal records; PAN cards, Aadhaar cards, vehicle registration, birth/death certificates.
CompaniesEmployee information (name, address, salary, position, joining date); project tracking; infrastructure, sales and investment records.
E-CommerceProduct details (price, quantity, quality, manufacturing date, seller); customer details (name, address, contact, orders).
AirlinesFlight details (arrival/departure times, fares, capacity, bookings); online and offline reservation tracking.
RailwaysTrain details (arrival/departure times, fares, capacity, bookings); online and offline reservation tracking.
TelecommunicationsCommunication network details, customer records, call records and bills; plan details and subscriptions.
HotelsGuest details (check-in/check-out, duration of stay, room number); tracking booked and available rooms; staff, menu and infrastructure details.

Topic 3 Applications That Can Be Developed Using Databases

▼
⭐ List of Database-Driven Applications
  • College / School Management Application
  • Online Reservation System (Airlines, Railways, Buses, Movies, Hotels)
  • Online Tutorials and Test Platform
  • Hospital Management Application
  • Company / HR Management Application
  • Bank Management Application
  • Online Shopping Application
  • Bill Payment and Bill Generation Application
  • Library Management Application
  • Real Estate Management Application
  • Inventory Control Application
  • Shop Management Application
  • Restaurant Management Application
  • Telecom Management Application
  • Insurance Management Application
📚 Chapters
☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

🗣️ IT 802 Class 12 — Part A: Unit 1 – Communication Skills IV

🗣️
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part A · Unit 1

Communication Skills – IV

Active Listening — importance, steps, and Interview Skills — Do's and Don'ts.

Topic 1 Communication — Definition & Active Listening

▼
📖 Communication The act of transferring meanings from one entity or group to another through signs, symbols and semiotic norms that are mutually understood.
⭐ Learning Objectives of Effective Communication
  • Development of Interpersonal Skills.
  • To express effectively and with maximum efficiency.
📖 Active Listening One of the most important abilities for good communication. It helps in building and maintaining connections, resolving issues, streamlining processes, and remembering important information.
⭐ Why Active Listening is Important in the Workplace
BenefitExplanation
Builds ConnectionsPeople feel more comfortable sharing information when they know they are being actively listened to.
Builds TrustPeople trust us more when they feel they can speak openly without interruption or judgment.
Identifies ProblemsActive listening helps identify difficulties others are having and discover solutions faster.

Topic 2 Four Steps of Active Listening

▼
⭐ CARC — Four Steps of Active Listening
StepNameDescription
CCONTACTConnect with the speaker — make eye contact, maintain open posture and give nonverbal responses.
AABSORBTake in all aspects of the spoken message — implicit, explicit and nonverbal cues. Do not judge or evaluate.
RREFLECTIVE FEEDBACKMirror or reflect back what you have heard to confirm your understanding.
CCONFIRMReceive confirmation from the speaker that you heard the message accurately. If not, restart the process.

Topic 3 Interview Skills — Do's and Don'ts

▼
📖 Interview Skills The ability to communicate effectively and engage with the interviewer — skills that are just as important as the qualifications on your resume.
✅ DO's — Before an Interview
  • Get a good night's sleep before the interview.
  • Do research about the company and role.
  • Eat a good breakfast.
  • Prepare questions to ask the interviewer beforehand.
  • Know who will be interviewing you and learn about their background.
  • Know your strengths and prepare a list of them.
  • Turn off your cell phone.
  • Prepare a solid list of references.
✅ DO's — During an Interview
  • Smile and use smooth, emphatic gestures.
  • Get the interviewer's name right and use it a few times.
  • Look the interviewer in the eye — eye contact is very important.
  • Take notes during the interview.
  • Let your achievements speak for you.
  • Take a moment before answering a question.
  • Sit up straight and ask questions.
✅ DO's — After an Interview
  • Let the interviewer decide when the interview is over.
  • Ask when you will hear back if the interviewer does not say.
  • Drop off or send a thank you note.
  • Follow up appropriately.
❌ DON'Ts
  • Don't be late — plan to arrive early.
  • Don't sit down until you are asked.
  • Don't lean on or put your elbows on the interviewer's desk.
  • Don't show nervousness by drumming fingers or cracking knuckles.
  • Don't talk too quickly.
  • Don't use words whose meaning you don't know.
  • Don't use slang.
📚 Chapters
☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

🧘 IT 802 Class 12 — Part A: Unit 2 – Self-Management Skills IV

🧘
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part A · Unit 2

Self-Management Skills – IV

Self-motivation — types, sources, Personality Types, Traits and Personality Disorders.

Topic 1 Self-Motivation

▼
📖 Self-Motivation The capacity to complete tasks without external pressure. Motivation is the inner need to do something.
⭐ Importance of Self-Motivation
  • Increases an individual's energy and activity level.
  • Directs an individual towards specific goals.
  • Results in initiation and persistence of specific activities.
  • Affects cognitive processes and learning strategies.
⭐ Types of Motivation
TypeDescription
Internal (Intrinsic) MotivationIndividual demonstrates a desire to do work without any external reward — driven by personal satisfaction.
External (Extrinsic) MotivationDriven by outside forces — performing an activity to obtain a reward or to avoid punishment.

Topic 2 Sources of Motivation and Inspiration

▼
⭐ Sources of Motivation
SourceHow it Motivates
MusicA good inspiring piece touches every heart and can help create miracles.
BooksExpand our horizon of thinking and help us visualize beyond our capacities.
ActivitiesEngaging in positive and skill-enhancing activities — competitions, games, simulations — keeps spirits high.
Expansive ThoughtsThinking and discussing big and positive ideas motivates us to reach our highest potential.
Living in the PresentBeing mindful — aware and conscious of the present moment — brings peace and calm. Also called mindfulness.
Dreaming BigDreaming big is a journey, not a destination. It prepares us mentally to take that big leap forward.

Topic 3 Personality Types, Traits & Disorders

▼
📖 Personality A rather stable collection of characteristics — a combination of emotional, behavioural and mental traits. Influenced by both heredity and environmental factors.
⭐ Big Five Personality Traits
TraitDescriptionOpposite
ExtraversionGregarious, assertive and sociableReserved, timid, quiet
AgreeablenessCooperative, warm and agreeableCold, disagreeable, antagonistic
ConscientiousnessHardworking, organized and dependableLazy, disorganized, unreliable
Emotional StabilityCalm, self-confident and coolInsecure, anxious, depressed
Openness to ExperienceCreative, curious and culturedNarrow interests, uncreative
⭐ Common Personality Disorders
DisorderDescription
ParanoidFeeling extremely nervous because you believe others do not like you or are trying to harm you.
AntisocialPersistent disregard for and violation of the rights of others.
BorderlineOngoing pattern of varying moods, self-image and behaviour leading to impulsive actions.
NarcissisticInflated sense of self-importance, need for excessive attention and lack of empathy.
AvoidantExtreme social inhibition, feelings of inadequacy and sensitivity to negative criticism.
DependentOverreliance on others to meet emotional and physical needs.
Obsessive (OCPD)Extreme perfectionism, order and neatness.
📚 Chapters
☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

💻 IT 802 Class 12 — Part A: Unit 3 – ICT Skills IV

💻
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part A · Unit 3

ICT Skills – IV (Spreadsheet Application)

OpenOffice Calc — Worksheet operations, Formatting, Filter, Sort, Formulas, Functions, Password Protection and Saving formats.

Topic 1 Spreadsheet Application — OpenOffice Calc

▼
📖 Spreadsheet Application Software that assists with formula computations and data analysis. OpenOffice Calc is a free, open-source spreadsheet tool included in the OpenOffice package.
⭐ Features of Spreadsheet Programs
  • Built-in functions make calculations easier, faster and more accurate.
  • Large volumes of data can be easily handled and manipulated.
  • Data can be exported to or imported from other software.
  • Data can be represented in pictorial form like graphs or charts.
  • Formulae are automatically recalculated whenever underlying data changes.
⭐ Components of Calc Screen
  • Title bar, Menu bar, Standard bar, Formatting bar
  • Name Box, Formula Bar, Worksheet tabs
⭐ Keyboard Shortcuts
ActionShortcut
New WorkbookCtrl + N
SaveCtrl + S
CopyCtrl + C
CutCtrl + X
PasteCtrl + V

Topic 2 Filter, Sorting & Cell Operations

▼
📖 Filtering A quick approach to locate and work with selected data. Only rows/columns that satisfy the defined criteria are displayed. Filtering temporarily hides unwanted data (does NOT delete it).
⭐ Types of Filter in Calc
Filter TypePathUse
AutoFilterData → Filter → AutoFilterQuick filter using dropdown arrows on column headers
Standard FilterData → Filter → Standard FilterConnect multiple conditions using logical AND/OR
📖 Sorting Organising data in a spreadsheet in ascending or descending order. Can sort on one column or multiple columns.
⭐ Row and Column Operations
OperationSteps
Insert RowSelect row → Insert menu → Rows (OR right-click row header → Insert Rows)
Delete RowSelect row → right-click → Delete Rows
Insert ColumnSelect column → Insert menu → Columns
Delete ColumnSelect column → right-click → Delete Columns
Change Row HeightFormat → Row → Height OR drag divider below row header
Change Column WidthFormat → Column → Width OR drag divider to right of column header

Topic 3 Formulas, Functions & Error Results

▼
📖 Formula Always begins with = (equal to sign) in Calc. Used to compute results using arithmetic operations. If = is omitted, it is treated as text.
⭐ Common Calc Functions
FunctionPurposeExample
SUM()Find total of a range=SUM(A1:A10)
AVERAGE()Find average of numbers=AVERAGE(A1:A10)
COUNT()Count numeric values in range=COUNT(A1:A10)
MAX()Find maximum value=MAX(A1:A10)
MIN()Find minimum value=MIN(A1:A10)
⭐ Common Error Results in Calc
ErrorReason
#####Column is not wide enough to display the value
#DIV/0!Formula contains division by zero
#VALUE!Formula has invalid argument (e.g. text where number is needed)

Topic 4 Password Protection & Saving Formats

▼
⭐ Password Protecting a Calc Document
MethodSteps
Using Tools MenuTools → Protect Document → Sheet or Document → Enter password → Click OK
While SavingFile → Save As → Select "Save with password" checkbox → Click Save → Enter password → Click OK
⭐ Saving in Different Formats
FormatHow to Save
ODS (Default)File → Save (Ctrl + S) — extension is .ods
Microsoft Excel (.xls)File → Save As → Change "Save as type" to Microsoft Excel 97/2000/XP (.xls)
PDFClick "Export Directly as PDF" icon on Standard bar → Select location → Click OK

☕ IT Class 12

🚀 IT 802 Class 12 — Part A: Unit 4 – Entrepreneurial Skills IV

🚀
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part A · Unit 4

Entrepreneurial Skills – IV

Barriers to entrepreneurship, Fear as a barrier, and Entrepreneurial Competencies.

Topic 1 Barriers to Becoming an Entrepreneur

▼
⭐ Major Barriers to Entrepreneurship
BarrierDescription
Unsupportive Business EnvironmentCartelization, government laws, corruption, bribery and bureaucracy may make the business climate unfriendly.
Market Entry RegulationsTaxes, licensing requirements, environmental standards and lending requirements create barriers to starting a business.
Shortage of CapitalLack of funds or trouble finding suitable investors — especially in developing nations — is a major hurdle.
Skilled Workforce ChallengesLack of practical skills, employee turnover, changes in labour laws and increasing financial expectations create barriers.
Lack of Entrepreneurial MindsetA culture that discourages entrepreneurial thinking becomes a hurdle to starting new businesses.
Risk AversionWhen people in a culture are risk-averse, entrepreneurs find it difficult to succeed.
⭐ Fear as a Barrier — Types
Type of FearDescription
Fear of FailureBelief that everything could go wrong. Best overcome by accepting that failure is part of the journey.
Fear of the UnknownAvoiding uncertainties and unknowns because we fear change and losing control.
Fear of Not Being an ExpertFeeling of not knowing enough about the industry or product.
Fear of Uncomfortable SituationsUncomfortable circumstances like public speaking make many people afraid.
Fear of Risk TakingConfusing dangerous (gambling) risk with calculated (strategic) risk.

Topic 2 Entrepreneurial Competencies

▼
📖 Entrepreneurial Competency Underlying personal traits that lead to efficient and exceptional performance in a business role. These can be acquired through training, experience and mentoring.
⭐ Key Entrepreneurial Competencies (McClelland & EDII Study)
CompetencyMeaning
Taking InitiativeTaking the first step toward starting a business and acting on ideas.
Seeking OpportunityConstantly looking for and seizing opportunities in the business's best interest.
PersistenceContinuously trying, adapting and iterating to overcome challenges.
Information SeekingAlways alert to new opportunities, seeking advice from mentors and professionals.
Concern for QualityHolding themselves to the highest standards and striving for perfection.
CommitmentHigh level of dedication to their work and decisions.
Systematic PlanningCreating relevant, realistic plans and ensuring they are carried out effectively.
Problem SolvingViews every issue as a challenge and finds the best solution.
Self-ConfidenceNot intimidated by challenges; complete faith in their competency.
Risk TakingTakes calculated (not reckless) risks, using risk management techniques.
CreativityMaking improvements, considering alternatives and continuously iterating.
Team BuildingUniting people and inspiring them to cooperate as a team.
Decision MakingAbility to make the right decisions at the proper time.
AdaptabilityModifying thoughts and behaviours in response to change or new information.
📚 Chapters
☕ IT Class 12
Unit 1: Communication Skills-IV Unit 2: Self-Management Skills-IV Unit 3: ICT Skills-IV Unit 4: Entrepreneurial Skills-IV Unit 5: Green Skills-IV Unit 1: Database – RDBMS Unit 2: Web Based Applications Unit 3: Java Programming Unit 4: Work Integrated Learning

🌱 IT 802 Class 12 — Part A: Unit 5 – Green Skills IV

🌱
CBSE Class XII · IT Code 802 · Part A · Unit 5

Green Skills – IV

Green Jobs Initiative, Key Drivers of Green Employment, and various Green Job categories.

Topic 1 Green Jobs — Introduction

▼
📖 Green Jobs Jobs in agriculture, industry, services and administration that help maintain or improve the environment. They help businesses reduce their environmental impact to sustainable levels.
⭐ Green Jobs Initiative (2008)

Jointly introduced in 2008 by: IEO (International Employers Organization), ITUC (International Trade Union Confederation), ILO (International Labor Organization) and UNEP (United Nations Environment Programme).

⭐ Key Drivers of Green Employment
  • Green innovation generates new jobs and preserves existing ones.
  • Growing need to make economies more sustainable and reduce environmental impact.
  • Threat of climate change requiring urgent action.
  • Need for dematerialization, remanufacturing, zero-waste systems and durable products.

Topic 2 Types of Green Jobs

▼
⭐ Green Job Categories
Green JobDescription
Urban GrowersOversee terrace gardens in cities, cultivating fruits and vegetables using environmentally responsible methods.
Clean Car EngineersInvestigate strategies for preventing car emissions of harmful gases and creating sustainable transportation modes.
Biofuel JobsWork with alternative fuels (biofuels) to power vehicles without causing pollution.
Building Roads with Plastic WasteEngineers building roads using scrap plastic — solving both road quality and garbage disposal problems.
Solar Cell TechnicianWork with solar panels — choosing inexpensive yet durable materials for solar cells.
Solar Panel Engineers & InstallerDesign and install solar panels for efficient power generation.
Wind Energy WorkersDevelop plans and systems to produce power from wind energy.
Wave Energy ProducersUtilize ocean wave energy to produce electricity without depleting resources.
Water Quality TechniciansMonitor and treat water due to rising water pollution levels.
Rainwater Harvesting EngineersDesign and build rainwater harvesting systems (RWHS) for buildings to collect and reuse rainwater.
Waste Water Treatment WorkersDesign systems in green buildings to treat and reuse waste water.
RecyclersSeparate and gather recyclable materials; many upcycle waste materials.
E-Waste RecyclersGather and recycle electronic debris from companies and industries.
Sewage Treatment WorkersTreat waste at sewage treatment plants using human engineering.
Eco-TourismFocus on environmentally sustainable travel to pristine natural areas.
Green BuildersEnsure environment-friendly materials are used in construction.
Energy AuditorsExamine structures, monitor energy use and identify energy leaks.
Energy ConsultantsHelp organizations analyze energy consumption and reduce it.
Environmental EngineersCombine engineering, biology and chemistry to offer environmental solutions.
Natural ScientistsTrack and evaluate how human actions affect the environment.
Environmental Education TeacherEducate young minds and foster acceptance of environmental conservation.
